title: The TRUE Origin Of Santa Claus Revealed
summary: This brief clip discusses the historical origins of Santa Claus, tracing the figure's evolution from semi-nomadic Sami reindeer herders through various European cultures to its modern American form. The speaker argues against the common assumption that Santa Claus derives primarily from Saint Nicholas.

key_claims.claim: Santa Claus tradition came to the U.S. through New York via the Dutch
The Dutch got their Santa Claus figure (Sinterklaas) from the Germans, who got it from Italians, who took it from Turkish tradition involving Saint Nicholas
The Turkish tradition derived from Slavic peoples, who got it from the Sami, a semi-nomadic reindeer herding people
Italians stole the grave of Saint Nicholas, who was Turkish
